title: Learn Python programming from thinking to real projects
subtitle: A programming foundation for students, absolute beginners, working adults and career switchers. Python Foundation now includes complete learner lessons, quizzes, code runner, projects and teacher guides.

codeCard.message: I can learn Python from zero
codeCard.messageMuted: I can learn Python from zero

badgeCard.label: Course status
badgeCard.title: Content completed
badgeCard.desc: Learner lessons, quizzes, code exercises, projects and teacher guides are ready.

pythonFoundation.title: Python Foundation is ready for learning and teaching
pythonFoundation.desc: The course follows a clear path from easy to hard: Input - Process - Output thinking, print, variables, input, data types, conditions, loops, lists, dictionaries, functions, debugging, file/data basics and final projects.
highlights: 5-10 quick quiz questions based on lesson difficulty
22 code exercises with automated tests and stdin input
Separate teacher guides for online/offline teaching

audienceSection.title: One Python foundation path for different learning goals
audienceSection.desc: Before learning, students can take the thinking test and placement test so teachers can choose the right pace, exercise level and guidance style.

students.title: Grades 6-12 students
students.desc: Build programming thinking, algorithmic habits, problem-solving skills and small products with Python.

beginners.title: Absolute beginners
beginners.desc: Start from zero with everyday examples, clear lessons, quick quizzes and an in-browser code runner.

workingAdults.title: Working adults
workingAdults.desc: Build a Python base before automation, file handling, table data and repetitive work tasks.

careerSwitchers.title: Career switchers
careerSwitchers.desc: Build structured programming fundamentals before choosing web, data, automation or AI.

pathSection.title: Start at the right point and follow a clear roadmap
pathSection.desc: Learners should go from thinking test to placement test, then Python Foundation, and later choose projects, automation, web or data/AI.

features: Entry tests for each learner group
Separate learner lessons and teacher guides
Code runner with stdin input and automated tests
Quizzes, projects, checklists and rubrics for progress assessment

learningPaths.title: 1. Programming Thinking Test
learningPaths.desc: Assess logic, patterns, conditions, algorithms, data reasoning and debugging mindset before coding.

learningPaths.title: 2. Python Foundation Placement Test
learningPaths.desc: Decide whether the learner should enter the main class, learn more slowly, or strengthen thinking first.

learningPaths.title: 3. Python Foundation
learningPaths.desc: A completed foundation course with 24 lessons, 178 quiz questions and 22 tested code exercises.

learningPaths.title: 4. Projects / Automation / Data
learningPaths.desc: After Python Foundation, learners can continue based on application or career goals.

methodSection.title: Learn through the cycle: understand - build - test - debug
methodSection.desc: Each lesson is designed for learners to read concise theory, view examples, enter stdin, run code, run tests, take a quiz and finish a small task.

steps.title: Understand
steps.desc: Read objectives, concepts and examples before writing code.

steps.title: Write code
steps.desc: Practice immediately with the in-browser code runner or VS Code.

steps.title: Run tests
steps.desc: Enter sample input, view real output and compare it with test cases.

steps.title: Wrap up
steps.desc: Complete the quiz, fix common mistakes and finish a small project.

cta.title: Start Python Foundation today
cta.desc: If the learner is unsure about pace, take the tests first. If ready, go directly to lesson 1 of Python Foundation.
